title: [lichen_l2.c:780-781] atomic_set(link_ctx_initialized, 0) before lichen_link_cleanup timing
description:
Category not checked: Atomic ordering and memory barriers

Bug found: In lichen_l2_enable() disable path, lines 780-781:
  atomic_set(&link_ctx_initialized, 0);
  lichen_link_cleanup(&link_ctx);

The atomic_set happens BEFORE cleanup. This means:
1. Thread A (disable): sets flag to 0
2. Thread B (RX callback): reads flag as 0, skips processing
3. Thread A (disable): calls cleanup

This is correct - the flag gates access, so clearing it first prevents new accesses.

BUT: What if Thread B already passed the flag check at line 1109 and is in the middle of lichen_link_rx() when Thread A clears the flag and starts cleanup?

Answer: This is handled by holding both tx_mutex and rx_mutex (lines 778-779) - Thread B must be holding rx_mutex to call lichen_link_rx(), so Thread A blocks until Thread B finishes.

Actually the sequence is:
1. Thread A: acquires tx_mutex, rx_mutex (lines 778-779)
2. Thread A: clears flag, calls cleanup
3. Thread A: releases mutexes

So any in-flight RX completes before cleanup. The code is correct.

Withdrawing this finding - the mutex acquisition makes the sequence safe.
